summary: Court-ordered financial recoveries are lumpy from year to year because they hinge on the outcome and timing of a small number of large cases rather than a steady flow of settlements. DOI's own reporting says the Fiscal 2023 spike to $10.1 million was driven almost entirely by one wire-fraud case in which two defendants were ordered to repay over $5 million, and that recovery amounts in any given year are largely outside DOI's control. The elevated Fiscal 2026 year-to-date figure reflects the same case-by-case volatility rather than a sustained trend.

sources.label: DOI, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing, though this is lumpy and driven by a handful of large cases
